symbols: kneeling king — Gentile nations worshipping Christ
stable/manger — humble birth of the King
tree — life or the Tree of Jesse
description: A Renaissance altarpiece depicting the Adoration of the Magi in an arched frame. The Virgin Mary sits at center-right holding the Infant Jesus, with Joseph standing behind. One of the Magi kneels before the Child, offering a gift. Other richly dressed figures gather at the left in a procession. The scene is set under a humble wooden structure with a lush tree behind and a landscape vista. The composition is balanced and harmonious, with soft colors and idealized figures characteristic of Perugino's Umbrian style.
theological_description: The Adoration of the Kings depicts Matthew 2:1-12, when wise men from the East came to worship the newborn King of the Jews. The Magi's journey, guided by a star, represents the revelation of Christ to the Gentile world — the Epiphany. Their gifts carry Christological significance: gold for His kingship, frankincense for His divinity, and myrrh anticipating His death and burial. The humble setting contrasts with the royal visitors, emphasizing that God's kingdom operates by a different logic than earthly power. This scene fulfills Isaiah's prophecy that nations would come bearing gold and frankincense to proclaim the Lord's praise (Isaiah 60:6).

scripture_refs.reason: The Magi from the East coming to worship the newborn King
Nations bringing gold and frankincense to proclaim the praise of the Lord
Kings bringing gifts and bowing down before the Messianic ruler
